definition: The value of the next-best alternative foregone when making a choice
The ability to produce a good at a lower opportunity cost than another producer; the basis for gains from trade
The percentage change in quantity demanded divided by the percentage change in price
The difference between what consumers are willing to pay and what they actually pay
The difference between the price a producer receives and the minimum price they would accept
The loss of total welfare that occurs when a market is not in equilibrium — typically caused by taxes
The cost of producing one additional unit of a good; the first derivative of the total cost function
A cost or benefit of an economic activity that affects a third party not involved in the transaction
